# Dedication {.unnumbered}

For the people who kept the reporting cycle alive long after the workbook should have died.

# About This Book {.unnumbered}

This book is written for professionals who never planned to become technical people and still ended up carrying technical work.

You might work in finance. Or HR. Or operations. Or reporting. Or business analysis. The title on your email signature is less important than the shape of your week. Files arrive. Formats drift. Lookups break. A dashboard needs updating before a meeting. Someone upstream changes a column name and half your morning disappears.

You know the work. You know the pressure. You know the strange fatigue of doing something familiar over and over and still not trusting the process.

This book was built for that reader.

Not the aspiring software engineer.

Not the hobbyist coder.

The working professional who is smart, capable, overloaded, and tired of babysitting spreadsheets.

## How to Read This Book {.unnumbered}

You do not need to read this like a programming manual.

Read it like a migration plan.

Each chapter moves from a familiar business pain point toward a more stable way of working. The story matters because burnout is part of the problem. The code matters because relief has to become operational.

Expect three things as you move through the book:

1.  Recognition. You will see your current work life on the page.
2.  Translation. Familiar Excel logic will be mapped into R in plain language.
3.  Transfer. You will leave each chapter with something you can use at work.

## What You Will Need {.unnumbered}

You need:

-   a laptop with R and RStudio installed
-   a few CSV or Excel files from work, with sensitive information removed if needed
-   the willingness to stop treating manual repetition as normal

## A Note on the Tone of This Book {.unnumbered}

This is a practical book with a human voice. It is not written to impress programmers. It is written to help professionals save time, reduce friction, and recover mental space.

## Optional Setup Check {.unnumbered}

The code blocks throughout the book are designed to be executable. This quick check gives the project a working starting point.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
version$version.string
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] "R version 4.5.2 (2025-10-31 ucrt)"
```


:::
:::


## Book Roadmap {.unnumbered}

The structure follows a professional transition arc:

-   first, you recognize the spreadsheet ceiling
-   then, you see why R fits this kind of work
-   then, you build confidence through small wins
-   then, you translate familiar Excel logic into repeatable workflows
-   finally, you start thinking like an R-powered strategist rather than a spreadsheet maintainer

## Publishing Note {.unnumbered}

This Quarto project is set up for HTML, PDF, and EPUB output. The same manuscript can become a book website, a print-ready interior, and a Kindle-friendly ebook with one project structure.
